CareerHub — Assignment 3.2

Applicant Profile · Document uploads · Auto-fill on apply · Resume where you stopped
Next.js 15 App Router React 19 TypeScript strict Tailwind v4 localStorage data layer

All screenshots below are real captures of the running app.

What's in here
  1. 1. What I built (summary)

1. What I built

A signed-in job-seeker now has a profile they fill in once, through a guided multi-step form:

  • Personal details — name, email, phone, ID number, date of birth, nationality, gender, and a profile photo.
  • Address — street, city, province (South African list), postal code, country.
  • Qualifications — four document slots uploaded as PDF: Matric certificate, ID copy, Driver's licence, Tertiary qualification.
  • Review — a read-back of everything with quick "Edit" jumps.

Everything is saved as the user goes, so the three headline requirements are met:

  • Stored on the profile — all the information lives in one profile record (per account).
  • Auto-fill on apply — the application form is pre-filled from the profile, and saved documents attach in one click, so you never start from the beginning.
  • Resume where you stopped — if you start an application (or the profile) and leave without finishing, your answers are saved and you're shown exactly where you stopped when you come back.

2. How the pieces fit together

One client-side data layer (src/lib/profile-store.ts, backed by localStorage — the same approach as the existing app) is the single source of truth. The profile wizard writes to it; the apply form reads from it for auto-fill and writes a draft back to it on every change.

  Profile wizard ───writes──►  profile-store (localStorage)
                                  │  profiles[email]
                                  │  drafts[email::jobId]
                                  ▼
  Apply form  ◄──auto-fill── reads profile
  Apply form  ───autosave──► writes draft   ──► Resume banner reads latest draft

FilePurpose
src/lib/profile-store.tsNEWProfile + draft data layer (localStorage): types, get/save, completion %, file→data-URL, draft get/save/clear.
src/app/profile/page.tsxNEWThe 4-step profile wizard with stepper, per-step save and resume-to-last-step.
src/components/profile/DocUpload.tsxNEWReusable PDF upload slot (validate, preview, view/replace/remove).
src/components/profile/ResumeBanner.tsxNEW"Unfinished application" banner driven by the latest saved draft.
src/app/apply/[jobId]/page.tsxCHANGEDAuto-fill from profile, attach saved documents, autosave + restore a draft, clear on submit.
src/components/Navbar.tsxCHANGEDAdded a "My profile" link for candidates (desktop + mobile menu).

4. The data layer — profile & drafts

The profile is one record per user, keyed by email. Files (photo + PDFs) are stored inline as base64 data URLs so they survive a reload with no backend. The shape:

export interface ApplicantProfile {
  email: string;            // owner key (lower-cased)
  photoDataUrl: string;     // profile picture (base64, "" when none)
  personal: PersonalDetails;
  address: Address;
  qualifications: Qualifications;  // matric / idCopy / license / tertiary
  completedSteps: number[];  // which wizard steps are done
  lastStep: number;          // where the user was last — used to resume
  createdAt: string; updatedAt: string;
}

Completion is derived (not stored), driving the progress bar and the "complete" gate:

export function profileCompletion(profile) {
  const personalDone = !!(p.fullName && p.email && p.phone && p.idNumber);
  const addressDone  = !!(a.line1 && a.city && a.province && a.postalCode);
  const qualificationsDone = REQUIRED_QUALIFICATIONS.every(k => quals[k] !== null);
  const percent = Math.round((done3 / 3) * 100);
  return { percent, personalDone, addressDone, qualificationsDone, complete };
}

Uploads go through one helper that enforces type + size and returns a stored doc:

export function fileToDataUrl(file, { accept, maxBytes }) {
  // reject if wrong type ("pdf" | "image") or over maxBytes,
  // else FileReader.readAsDataURL → { fileName, dataUrl, size, uploadedAt }
}

5. The profile wizard (the UI)

A four-step stepper. Each "Save & continue" persists the step and remembers it as lastStep, so a returning user lands back on the right step automatically.

The step is saved like this — the current step is marked complete and the next becomes lastStep:

function persist(current, nextStep) {
  setProfile((p) => {
    const completedSteps = [...new Set([...p.completedSteps, current])].sort();
    return saveProfile({ ...p, completedSteps, lastStep: nextStep });
  });
}
// On load we resume to where they stopped:
setStep(Math.min(savedProfile.lastStep, STEPS.length - 1));

6. Photo & PDF uploads

The four qualification slots reuse one controlled DocUpload component. It validates (PDF, ≤ 4 MB), shows the uploaded state, and offers View / Replace / Remove. The profile photo uses the same fileToDataUrl helper with accept: "image".

<DocUpload
  label={QUALIFICATION_LABELS[key]}              // e.g. "Matric certificate"
  hint={REQUIRED_QUALIFICATIONS.includes(key) ? "(required)" : "(optional)"}
  value={qualifications[key]}
  onChange={(doc) => setQualification(key, doc)} // null clears it
/>

7. Auto-fill when applying

When a job-seeker with a profile opens an application, the form is pre-filled from their profile and their saved documents can be attached with a single checkbox — so they never re-type anything.

// One-time load on the apply page:
const prof = getProfile(user.email);
if (prof) {
  setFullName(prof.personal.fullName || user.name);
  setEmail(prof.personal.email || user.email);
  setPhone(prof.personal.phone);
  setNationality(prof.personal.nationality);
  setIdNumber(prof.personal.idNumber);
  setPrefilled(true);   // shows the "pre-filled from your profile" banner
}

8. Resume where you stopped

Every change on the apply form is written to a per-job draft. If the user leaves without submitting, the next time they sign in the profile page greets them with an "unfinished application" banner, and reopening the form restores their answers. The draft is cleared on a successful submit.

The autosave only writes once something actually changes (a fresh, untouched pre-fill never creates a draft):

useEffect(() => {
  if (!user || !job || submitted || alreadyApplied) return;
  const snapshot = JSON.stringify({ fullName, email, phone, ... });
  if (snapshot === baselineRef.current) return;   // nothing changed yet
  saveDraft({ jobId: job.id, jobTitle: job.title, company: job.company,
              email: user.email, values: { ...current fields } });
}, [user, job, submitted, alreadyApplied, fullName, email, ...]);

// On submit:
saveApplication(application);
clearDraft(user.email, job.id);   // draft gone → banner clears

9. How to use it

1
Sign in as a candidate, then open My profile from the top bar.
2
Work through Personal → Address → Qualifications, uploading your photo and the four PDFs. Each step saves as you go.
3
Open any job and click Apply — the form is already filled from your profile; tick "Attach my saved documents".
4
If you close the tab mid-application, just sign back in: the profile page shows your unfinished application and reopening it restores everything.
